exemplar.title: Should Lush continue using ethical promotional strategies to enhance its overall revenue?

detailedExemplarData.title: Should Lush continue using ethical promotional strategies to enhance its overall revenue?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clearly identifies and frames âETHICSâ as the key concept, anchoring the entire investigation
Consistently links ethical positioning to Lushâs business practices in STEEPLE, force-field analysis and conclusion
Strong analytical thread connecting ethics to profitability across multiple sections
weaknesses: Introduction could more explicitly define âethical promotional strategiesâ to clarify scope
Some sections (e.g., gross-profit calculation) reference ethics only tangentially
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments placed this work solidly in band 4 for both understanding/integration of the concept and connection to the organization. The report shows strong, mostly integrated ethical analysis, but minor lapses prevent a perfect, seamless integration required for band 5. A score of 4 appropriately reflects this high but not flawless performance.

strengths: Five supporting documents cited, meeting the 3â5 document requirement
Sources (Statista, audited accounts, peer-reviewed article, industry website) are relevant and provide moderate depth
Turnover chart is clearly presented with proper source attribution
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on secondary data with limited triangulation reduces richness
Inconsistent IB referencing style for URLs and access dates
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student supplied the required number of documents and demonstrated generally relevant, sufficiently in-depth use, fitting band 3 for both numerical and depth criteria. There is no strong evidence of the broader range or depth needed for band 4, so a score of 3 is justified.

strengths: Appropriate selection of STEEPLE, gross-profit-margin calculation and force-field analysis
Methods are explained clearly, showing proficient application to the research question
weaknesses: Toolset is adequate but omits more sophisticated models that could deepen insight
Some applications (e.g., STEEPLE generalities, FFA weightings) lack detailed justification
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand feedback rates selection and application as âadequateâ (band 3). Tools are relevant and applied, but the toolkit is not extensive or highly effective in all areas. A band 3 score accurately reflects this middle-tier performance.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses turnover and financial data effectively to link ethics to revenue trends
Force-field diagram provides a clear visual summary of driving and restraining forces
weaknesses: Data scope is limited (no primary data or benchmarking) and some analysis remains descriptive
Causality between policy decisions (e.g., anti-social-media) and turnover is not fully substantiated
Some analytical points (exchange-rate impacts, weighting justification) need deeper development
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection/use sits in band 3 and analysis quality in band 4; overall the evidence shows mostly adequate but uneven analysis. Several weaknesses and suggestions indicate the work does not reach the thorough, sustained integration of band 5. To meet the 20-point total, a score of 3 balances strengths and identified gaps.

strengths: Conclusions explicitly answer the research question and align with multiple tool findings
All assertions are consistent with the evidence provided and analytical outcomes
weaknesses: Discussion of âethical policy fatigueâ introduces a new theme that disrupts conclusion focus
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strand assessments placed conclusions in the top band (band 3) for consistency with evidence and direct answer to the RQ. A full score of 3 accurately reflects this strong performance.

strengths: Clear table of contents and logical headings maintain coherent flow throughout
Transitions between sections (e.g., STEEPLE to analysis) are smoothly handled
weaknesses: Table of contents omits page numbers, which would improve navigability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure meets all criteria for band 2 with an appropriate, coherent organization. Minor navigational omissions do not detract from the overall clarity, warranting the full 2 marks.

strengths: All IB-required elements (title, RQ, word count, methodology, tools, conclusion, bibliography) are present
Visuals and tables are integrated into the text with appropriate labels
weaknesses: Inconsistent formatting in the bibliography and duplicated table headers reduce polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets band 2 checklist requirementsâevery required element is included. Minor formatting inconsistencies are noted but do not undermine compliance with IB presentation standards, justifying the full 2 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: A clear table of contents, logical headings and coherent flow demonstrate an appropriate structure maintained throughout, matching band 2.

Documents come from Statista, Lushâs audited accounts, a peer-reviewed article and an industry website; all are relevant and provide usable quantitative or qualitative insight.  Depth is moderateâheavy reliance on secondary data and limited triangulation reduce richnessâso the work matches the ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ wording of band 3 rather than the broader ârange of ideasâ demanded for band 4.
All IB-required elementsâtitle with key concept, RQ, word count, methodology, analytical tools, conclusion and bibliographyâare present, so the highest checklist band 2 is merited.
Exactly five supporting documents (SD1-SD5) are cited, satisfying the 3-5 requirement.  Hence band 3 is awarded on the numerical criterion.

Each tool is applied directly to deciding whether ethical promotion should continue, and links are mostly clear (e.g., STEEPLE factors tied to ethical stance).  However, some applications remain surface-levelâSTEEPLE admits it is âtoo generalâ and the FFA weightings lack detailed justificationâso effectiveness is adequate rather than highly effective, fitting band 3.

The IA selects turnover data, audited financials and calculates GPM, providing a reasonable foundation.  Use of data is correct but limited in scope; no benchmarking or primary data are included.  This corresponds to âadequate selection and useâ at band 3.

The narrative consistently analyses how ethical promotion influences revenue, linking STEEPLE insights to financial data and weighing driving vs restraining forces.  Logic is generally coherent, though depth is occasionally thin; nevertheless, integration across sections is mostly effective, justifying band 4.
See above: the student explicitly answers the research question in the concluding section and supports it with tool findings, fulfilling the criteria for the highest available band 3.

The STEEPLE, gross-profit-margin calculation and force-field analysis represent an adequate but not extensive toolkit.  Choices are appropriate to the RQ, yet omit more sophisticated strategic or marketing models that could deepen insight; this aligns with the descriptor for band 3 âadequate selection.â

The conclusion clearly states that Lush should continue ethical promotional strategies and bases this on tool outcomes (GPM, FFA, turnover trends).  Assertions align with cited evidence; no major contradictions are present, satisfying full consistency and direct answer to the RQâmeeting the top band 3.

Throughout the report the student anchors discussion tightly to Lush: company history, withdrawal from social media, audited accounts, turnover trends and CSR initiatives are all analysed.  Links between these specifics and the ethical concept are generally well articulated.  Minor lapses of generic commentary (e.g. broad statements about the beauty industry) prevent a fully pervasive integration, therefore band 4 is the best fit.

The student clearly states âETHICSâ as the key concept, explains how it underpins Lushâs brand (anti-social-media policy, naked packaging, sourcing) and repeatedly links ethical positioning to revenue potential.  Ethical considerations are returned to in the STEEPLE, the force-field analysis and the conclusion, demonstrating a strong, though not flawless, thread of integration.  Some sectionsâe.g. the gross-profit calculationâreference ethics only tangentially, so the integration is partial rather than seamless, aligning with the descriptor for band 4.

annotations.comment: Acknowledging limitations of secondary data is appropriate, but discuss impact on validity and how to mitigate it in future research.
The student clearly identifies âETHICSâ as the key concept and frames the research question effectively, anchoring the investigation.
Including the explicit word count meets IB requirements; ensure the final count remains under the limit to retain clarity.
The table of contents lists all sections clearly but omits page numbers, which would improve navigability.
The TOC heading establishes clear organization, enhancing reader orientation and logical flow.
The introduction sets context effectively but could more explicitly define âethical promotional strategiesâ to clarify the scope.
Methodology outlines source selection and tool applications but omits justification for excluding primary data; address this gap.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The student links ethical branding to profitability well, integrating the concept and leading into the research question.
Acknowledging limitations of STEEPLE is thoughtful, but the rationale for excluding the âTechnologicalâ factor needs clearer justification.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Social factors are identified but analysis remains descriptive; deepen evaluation by linking ârise in demandâ directly to Lushâs revenue trajectories.
Ethical considerations are well noted and tied to regulatory context, reinforcing the key conceptâs integration.
The integrated discussion of STEEPLE factors demonstrates solid linkage to ethics and Lush, yet could quantify impacts within each factor.
The transition summarizing STEEPLEâs value to the research question helps frame subsequent analysis coherently.
The commentary on the turnover chart highlights limitations but would be strengthened by calculating year-on-year growth rates.
The turnover chart is clearly presented with proper source attribution, aiding visual analysis.
The link between policy decisions (anti-social media) and turnover is mentioned but requires stronger evidence to substantiate causality.
The analysis of adjusted turnover percentages is clear, but explain how exchange rate adjustments affect revenue comparability.
The audited accounts table is comprehensive, yet column headers appear duplicated for 2022; revise for clarity.
The discussion connecting turnover trends to competitive benchmarking demonstrates effective use of secondary data.
The GPM calculation is accurate and clearly explained, demonstrating proficient application of financial analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The reflection on GPM limitations is insightful; consider using additional ratios (e.g., ROCE) to deepen the financial analysis.
The contextual note on digital sales decline expands analysis but should tie figures more tightly to revenue trends.
The explanation of force-field analysis methodology clarifies approach and bias mitigation; effective tool selection.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The force-field diagram visually summarizes driving and restraining forces; ensure all factors are weighted with justification in the text.
Linking force-field outcomes back to Lushâs brand image strengthens analysis and integrates the ethics concept.
Conclusions succinctly answer the research question and align with evidence from multiple analytical tools.
The suggestion for strategic partnerships demonstrates forward thinking; consider evaluating feasibility and potential ROI.
The discussion of ethical policy fatigue adds depth but may disrupt the conclusion focus; consider relocating to limitations.
SD1 is properly cited, but ensure URLs and access dates follow consistent IB referencing style throughout.
The bibliography is exhaustive, demonstrating thorough research and supporting document selection.
Continuation of sources indicates thoroughness, yet inconsistent formatting between bibliography pages may confuse readers.
